# GitHub Copilot review instructions — gitops-tenant-template

`devantler-tech/gitops-tenant-template` is a **GitHub template for a GitOps tenant
on the devantler-tech platform**. A tenant is a **private** application deployed to
the platform Kubernetes cluster as an **OCI-packaged Kustomize app**, reconciled by
Flux CD. It is **not** a general code repo — the deliverable is Kubernetes YAML plus
the CI/CD plumbing that builds, signs, and publishes it. Enforce the rules below when
reviewing; they complement `AGENTS.md` (the canonical, cross-tool instructions) —
keep both in sync, and if a PR changes a convention here it updates `AGENTS.md` too.

## Scope & altitude
- Review manifests **statically** (`kubectl kustomize deploy/`, schema/policy) —
  never assume a PR is applied to a live cluster as part of review.
- A tenant's app lives in `deploy/` as Kustomize manifests: a Deployment, Service,
  HTTPRoute, an optional CloudNativePG `Cluster`, and — when secrets are needed — a
  namespaced `SecretStore` + `ExternalSecret`. Flag manifests that break
  `kubectl kustomize` or reference resources the overlay doesn't define.

## Secrets — External Secrets + OpenBao only
- Tenant secrets are sourced at runtime from **OpenBao** via a namespaced
  **`SecretStore` + `ExternalSecret`** (External Secrets Operator) — **never** a
  committed `Secret`. **Flag any plaintext secret, credential, token, private key,
  or unencrypted `Secret` data** in the repo, and any `ExternalSecret` that points
  outside the tenant's own OpenBao path.

## Template-owned vs tenant-owned
- **Template-owned shared plumbing** (changed only upstream in this template, never
  in a tenant): `.github/workflows/{cd,release,template-sync,validate-scaffold,sync-labels}.yaml`,
  `CLAUDE.md`, `zizmor.yml`, `scripts/rename-placeholders.sh`, and this file.
- **Tenant-owned** files are listed in `.templatesyncignore` (`AGENTS.md`,
  `README.md`, `LICENSE`, `deploy/`, `Dockerfile`, `ci.yaml`, `dependabot.yml`,
  `.releaserc`, …). Flag a PR that edits shared plumbing in a way a tenant would
  lose on the next template-sync, or that removes a `.templatesyncignore` entry
  without reason.

## Commits, CI & security
- **PR titles must be Conventional Commits** (`feat:`/`fix:`/`chore:`/`docs:`/
  `ci:`/`refactor:`/`test:`) — semantic-release squash-merges the title into the
  release, so a non-conventional or bracket-prefixed title corrupts it. Flag
  violations.
- The `ci.yaml` PR gate stays green by **root-cause fixes only** — never a skipped
  or `continue-on-error` check, a weakened assertion, `--no-verify`, or a silenced
  linter to dodge red CI.
- Workflow changes must pass `actionlint`; pin third-party actions to a full-length
  commit SHA and set least-privilege `permissions:`. Flag unpinned actions and
  over-broad token scopes.
- This scaffolds **private** tenant apps — keep repo contents conservative and never
  expose them publicly.
- PRs from the Daily AI Assistant carry a `> 🤖 Generated by the Daily AI Assistant`
  line — expected, not a finding.

Keep this file concise (≤ 4000 chars — Copilot review truncates beyond that) and in
sync with `AGENTS.md`.
